original_abstract: The rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has popularized In-Context Learning (ICL) as a transformative paradigm in natural language processing. In this framework, models perform specialized tasks by leveraging a few-shot context rather than undergoing traditional weight updates. Given the growing importance of ICL in evaluating and extending the capabilities of LLMs, this paper provides a comprehensive survey of the current landscape, emerging progress, and persistent challenges in the field. We begin by establishing a formal definition of ICL and situating it within the broader context of related learning methodologies. We then categorize and evaluate state-of-the-art techniques, ranging from innovative training strategies to sophisticated prompt engineering and theoretical analyses. Furthermore, we explore a diverse array of application scenarios, including data engineering and dynamic knowledge updating. Finally, we highlight the inherent limitations of ICL and propose promising avenues for future investigation. Through this work, we aim to provide a foundational resource that inspires further research into the underlying mechanisms and optimization of In-Context Learning.
humanized_abstract: In-Context Learning (ICL) has been identified as a promising paradigm for large language models (LLMs) due to the recent speed of advancements in LLMs. ICL enables models to perform specific tasks using a few-shot context in a manner that is different from the few-shot learning by updating the model parameters. With the increasing relevance of ICL to the assessment and the extension of the LLMs’ capabilities, this paper surveys ICL from all aspects, including current research developments, challenges, and more. Specifically, we introduce ICL in a formal way and highlight its relationship to other learning methods, and further classify and evaluate numerous existing researches on ICL methods, such as the training methods, the prompt engineering, and theories. Additionally, we introduce several use cases such as data engineering and dynamic knowledge injection. Finally, we summarize the limitations of ICL and suggest the directions for future work. This survey aims to be the basis of further research into the mechanics and optimization of ICL.
